title: NOC 12113 – Statistical Officers and Related Research Support Occupations | GTR Immigration
description: Statistical Officers and Research Support Professionals play a critical role in data analysis, reporting, and evidence-based decision-making across Canada’s public and private sectors. Classified under NOC 12113, this occupation benefits from steady post-pandemic demand and provides viable immigration pathways toward Permanent Residence (PR) through Canada’s skilled worker programs and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations
classification.minor_group: 1211 – Court reporters, transcriptionists, records management technicians, and statistical officers

common_job_titles: Research assistant – generalist (except university) Research support officer Social research assistant (except at university) Statistical officer Technical research assistant (except post-secondary)
duties: Statistical Officers and Research Support Professionals may:
Compile tables, charts, graphs, and summarized statistics for reports
Process data using statistical software to conduct trend analyses
Collect, consolidate, and cross-tabulate datasets from multiple sources
Prepare draft reports for review by researchers or supervisors
Track service-use data and provide management reports
Supervise junior research or statistical staff
employment_requirements: A college diploma or undergraduate degree in social science, commerce, statistics, or a related field
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 10,500 new positions for Statistical Officers and Related Research Support Occupations, compared with 10,600 job seekers—making careful province selection and strategic immigration planning especially important for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Statistical Officers and Research Support staff are employed across government agencies, research organizations, consulting firms, healthcare institutions, and corporations nationwide.

Growing reliance on data-driven research and analyticsStable hiring in government and consulting sectorsEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported work permit opportunitiesAnnual earnings typically ranging from $35,700 to $91,800 CAD
